_meta.name: EXHIBIT Design Foundations
_meta.description: The complete design rulebook for AI-assisted UI work. Fetch this file at the start of any UI task to guarantee visual consistency, correct typography, and professional output.
_meta.usage: Pass this file as context before asking an AI to build any dashboard, form, layout, or component. It defines what 'correct' looks and feels like.

typography._rule: Never mix font families except as specified. Never use system-ui or Arial for product UI. Body text is always Inter. Display/headings use Space Grotesk. Code/data uses JetBrains Mono.

display.usage: Page titles, hero headlines, section headings, card titles, dashboard H1/H2

body.usage: All prose, labels, descriptions, table content, form text, nav items, captions

mono.usage: Code snippets, slugs, IDs, numeric data in tables, timestamps, API responses

xs.usage: Table column headers (uppercase), captions, badge labels, timestamps

sm.usage: Body text, nav links, table rows, form labels, secondary info

base.usage: Primary body text, card descriptions, input values

md.usage: Section introductions, prominent labels, sidebar headings

lg.usage: Card titles, dialog headings, sub-page titles

xl.usage: Page section headings, KPI metric values

500.usage: Labels, nav items, table data, form field values

600.usage: Headings, button labels, card titles, badge text

700.usage: KPI values, page titles, display headings

800.usage: Logo text, hero headlines on marketing pages only

antiPatterns: Never set font-size in raw px without a corresponding Tailwind class equivalent
Never use font-weight 800+ for body text or UI labels
Never mix more than 3 font weights in a single component
Never ALL-CAPS body copy or descriptions (uppercase is OK for 10-11px table headers only)
Never use line-height below 1.25 for any readable text
Never use font-weight 400 for interactive elements (buttons, links, nav items)

colors._rule: Use the neutral palette as the foundation. Only add accent color for interactive, semantic, or brand moments. Never use raw color names (red, blue) — use the semantic palette.

subtle.usage: Table headers, hover states, inactive tabs

dark.usage: Dark mode page background, code editors

antiPatterns: Never use raw blue (#0000ff) for links or buttons — use the semantic link color
Never use color as the only differentiator — always pair with text, icon, or weight
Never use rgba backgrounds on solid white surfaces — use the neutral palette instead
Never exceed 3 distinct background colors in a single component
Never place light-colored text on neutral-100 or lighter backgrounds (contrast fails)

spacing._rule: All spacing uses a 4px base unit. Never use arbitrary values outside the scale. Card padding is always 16px (compact), 24px (default), or 32px (spacious). Page padding is 24px mobile, 32px tablet, 48px desktop.

inlineGaps.comfortable: 12px between related elements in a row

antiPatterns: Never use odd spacing values (5px, 7px, 9px) — always multiples of 4
Never use margin-top and margin-bottom together on the same element — pick one
Never collapse section padding below 16px on any surface

wide.usage: Sidebar with descriptions or secondary nav

sm.usage: Compact table rows, badges, small chips

2xl.usage: Mobile cards, hero cards, prominent features

full.usage: Pills, avatars, toggle buttons, status dots

antiPatterns: Never mix border-radius sizes within a single card or component
Never create sidebars wider than 320px without a specific need
Never place content directly against viewport edges — always use page padding

elevation._rule: Use the minimum elevation needed. Most surfaces should be flat (level 0). Only lift elements that need to stand above the page.

0.usage: Flat panels, table rows, sections on white

1.usage: Cards on neutral backgrounds, lifted panels

antiPatterns: Never apply shadow to elements already inside a card with a shadow
Never use shadow-xl on flat list items or table rows
Never mix box-shadow and border on the same element unless intentional (e.g., cards)

rules: Always font-weight 500 minimum
Minimum width 80px for any button
Icon buttons must have a tooltip or aria-label
Destructive actions must use the danger variant, not just red text
Never use outline buttons as the primary CTA

inputs.focusRing: 2px ring at 10% opacity of the accent color
rules: Every input must have a visible label (never rely on placeholder as label)
Error state: border-red-400 + red helper text below
Success state: border-emerald-400 only (no icon needed)
Disabled state: bg-neutral-50 + text-neutral-400 + cursor-not-allowed

tables.headerStyle: text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-neutral-500, bg-neutral-50
tables.borders: border-b border-neutral-100 between rows, border-neutral-200 around table
rules: Always right-align numeric columns
Never show more than 8 columns without horizontal scroll
Status columns use semantic badge pills (never raw text)
Action columns (edit/delete) go in the rightmost column
Empty state inside table should explain why and what action to take

badges.textCase: Sentence case for tags, UPPERCASE for status codes only

avatars.rule: Always rounded-full. Use gradient backgrounds for initials. Never use a square avatar.

cards.shadow: shadow-sm on neutral-50 backgrounds, none on white
rules: Never nest cards more than 1 level deep
Cards on a white page background should have a border AND shadow-sm
Cards inside a panel (bg-neutral-50) need only a border

density._rule: Pick one density mode per layout and use it consistently throughout. Never mix compact and comfortable in the same view.

compact.usage: Data-heavy tables, admin panels, dense analytics, spreadsheet-like views
compact.example: Revenue reconciliation tables, log viewers, permission matrices

default.usage: Standard product dashboards, settings pages, CRM views
default.example: User tables, order lists, inbox threads

comfortable.usage: Marketing dashboards, consumer apps, onboarding flows, mobile views
comfortable.example: Activity feeds, profile sections, pricing cards

rules: KPI label: 11-12px, text-neutral-500, font-medium
KPI value: 24-28px, font-bold, text-neutral-900
Trend: 12px, font-semibold, emerald-600 for positive, red-500 for negative
Never show more than 4 KPI cards in a single row without scrolling
Always include a chart title — never a chart without context
Bar charts: right-align y-axis labels, use neutral-200 grid lines
Line charts: 2px stroke width, dot on last data point
Pie/donut: always show legend to the right or below
Never just show 'No data found' — explain why and what to do
Empty table: center the message in the table body rows, not outside the table
Empty search: distinguish between 'no results' and 'nothing exists yet'

antiPatterns._description: These are the most common mistakes AI-generated UI makes. Avoid all of them.
typography: Raw browser default fonts (Times New Roman, system-ui without specifying Inter/Space Grotesk)
Unstyled h1/h2/h3 tags — always specify font-size, weight, and color
Mixed font sizes on the same level (some labels 12px, others 14px)
Long text blocks with line-height: 1 or less
ALL CAPS for descriptions or body content
layout: Content touching the viewport edge with no padding
Inconsistent gutter widths within the same grid
Deeply nested divs with redundant padding
Fixed pixel heights on containers that should expand with content
Missing max-width on reading columns (text spanning full 1400px)
color: Raw blue links (#0000ff or browser default blue)
Red/green only with no text label for status (color-blind failure)
Low contrast gray text on gray backgrounds
Multiple competing accent colors in the same component
Using background color to group elements that should use borders or whitespace
interaction: Unlabeled icon buttons (no tooltip, no aria-label)
Form submission with no loading state and no error handling display
Modals without a visible close button or ESC key support
Dropdowns that extend outside the viewport
dataDisplay: Charts without titles or axis labels
Status text in black without semantic color treatment
Empty tables showing nothing instead of an empty state message
More than 8 columns in a table without horizontal scroll
rawStyle: Unstyled HTML select elements (use a custom dropdown component)
Browser-default radio buttons and checkboxes (style them)
Tables with no border-collapse, raw border styling, or alternating gray rows without intent
Forms with inconsistent input heights in the same form
Mixing outline and solid button styles randomly

targetReference._description: This is what 'done right' looks like. Use these components as anchors.

fullDashboard.description: The canonical example of a complete, polished admin dashboard. Every element follows these foundations. Use it as the visual target.

tokenReference.description: Visual showcase of every color, type size, spacing value, and elevation level from this system.

antiPatternContrast.description: Side-by-side showing common raw/broken UI vs the corrected version. Useful for understanding what NOT to do.
